title: SMS Inbox & Messaging
lede: Every text your business sends and receives — customer replies, technician confirmations, group threads, even your AI agent's conversations — lands in one searchable SMS inbox. This guide takes you A-to-Z: reading threads, filtering like a pro, replying with one-click templates, and starting brand-new conversations.

steps.title: Open the SMS inbox
steps.content:
Your entire texting operation lives on one screen. Let's find it.

  1. In the top navigation, click Messages, then the SMS MESSAGES tab (or in the left sidebar under MESSAGES, click SMS Messages).
  2. The Messages page opens with three zones:
  • Header row — the page title with a red unread counter badge, a Search messages... box that searches names, numbers, and message text, a Refresh icon, and the green New Message button.
  • Filter bar — channel, direction, sort, and date controls (covered in the next steps).
  • Two-panel workspace — the conversation list on the left, and the full message thread of whichever conversation you select on the right.
💡 New messages appear automatically in real time — you don't need to refresh. The unread badge in the header updates the moment a customer texts you.

images.alt: The SMS Messages inbox with the filter bar, conversation list on the left and the open thread on the right
images.caption: The SMS inbox — conversations on the left, the selected thread on the right.

images.alt: The SMS Messages inbox in dark mode with the filter bar, conversation list and open thread
images.caption: The SMS inbox — conversations on the left, the selected thread on the right.

steps.title: Read a conversation card at a glance
steps.content:
Each card in the left-hand list is a full conversation with one person (or one group). The card packs a lot of signal into a small space:

  • Name or number — if the phone number matches a lead, contact, or technician in your CRM, their name shows automatically. Unknown numbers show as the raw number until you save them as a CRM record.
  • Timestamp — when the last message was sent or received.
  • Preview line — the first words of the most recent message.
  • Direction arrow — a green arrow for the last message being inbound (they texted you) or red for outbound (you texted them). Hover it for the exact direction.
  • Channel chip — SMS (blue) or WhatsApp (green), since this inbox unifies both text channels.
  • Message count — total messages in the thread.
  • Unread badge — a red count when the thread has messages you haven't opened yet.

Group MMS threads (multiple people on one text thread) show a purple team icon and list the participants — hover the name row to see the full roster. Threads that came in through a job source also show a small Source tag.

💡 The linking is automatic: because iMash matches phone numbers against your CRM, texting with a lead shows their name here, and their thread shows up when you filter by that lead — no manual tagging needed.

images.alt: A single conversation card showing the number, timestamp, preview, direction arrow, SMS channel chip and message count
images.caption: One conversation card: name/number, last-message time, preview, direction arrow, channel chip, and total count.

images.alt: A single conversation card in dark mode showing the number, timestamp, preview, direction arrow, SMS chip and message count
images.caption: One conversation card: name/number, last-message time, preview, direction arrow, channel chip, and total count.

steps.title: Filter and sort the inbox
steps.content:
When texts are flying, the filter bar keeps you focused. Left to right:

  • Channel — All, SMS, or WhatsApp. This one inbox carries both channels; pick one to isolate it.
  • Direction — All, Outbound, or Inbound.
  • Sort — Sort: Newest, Sort: Oldest, or Sort: Most unread (bubbles the threads that need attention to the top).
  • Date preset — Today, Yesterday, or Last 7 Days, plus a custom date-range picker next to it for any period. Click the small × in the range picker to clear the date filter and see everything.

Every active filter also appears as a removable chip next to the page title — click a chip's × to drop just that filter, or Clear all to reset the whole bar.

⚠️ The inbox opens with Last 7 Days pre-selected. If a conversation seems to be "missing", clear the date filter first — it's almost always just outside the window.

images.alt: The filter bar with channel, direction, sort, date preset, date range, More filters and Bulk buttons
images.caption: The filter bar: channel, direction, sort and date at a glance — More filters and Bulk on the second row.

images.alt: The filter bar in dark mode with channel, direction, sort, date preset, More filters and Bulk buttons
images.caption: The filter bar: channel, direction, sort and date at a glance — More filters and Bulk on the second row.

steps.title: Drill deeper with More filters
steps.content:
Click More filters to open the refinements popover. A count badge on the button reminds you when any of these are silently active.

  • Entity — narrow to conversations with Technicians, Contacts, Leads, or Other / Unlinked numbers. Picking a type reveals a second dropdown to zero in on one specific person — perfect for pulling up every text with one customer.
  • Delivery Status — show only threads whose messages were delivered, pending, or failed.
  • From Number (10DLC) — if you text from multiple business lines, filter to threads on a specific number.
  • Source — appears when your phone numbers are bound to job sources; filters to threads that arrived through one lead source.
  • Unread only — a switch that hides everything you've already read.
  • Has media — a switch that shows only threads containing photos or attachments (MMS).

Click Clear refinements at the bottom of the popover to reset just these advanced filters.

images.alt: The More filters popover with Entity, Delivery Status, From Number, Unread only and Has media controls
images.caption: More filters: entity, delivery status, business line, and the Unread only / Has media switches.

images.alt: The More filters popover in dark mode with Entity, Delivery Status, From Number, Unread only and Has media controls
images.caption: More filters: entity, delivery status, business line, and the Unread only / Has media switches.

steps.title: Open a thread and read it like a chat
steps.content:
Click any conversation card and the full thread opens on the right, newest messages at the bottom — just like your phone.

  • Thread header — the person's name and number on the left; on the right, via shows which of your business numbers this thread runs on, and any Associated Jobs appear as blue tags — click one to jump straight to that job's detail page.
  • Message bubbles — your outbound messages on the right (blue), inbound on the left. Under each outbound bubble you'll see the delivery state: ✓ sent, ✓✓ delivered, blue ✓✓ read.
  • Failed messages — flagged with a red FAILED tag. Hover the ! icon to read the carrier's error, then click Retry to resend it instantly.
  • Hover actions — hover any bubble to reveal Reply (quote that message in your response), Forward (send it to someone else), and on WhatsApp threads, React with an emoji.
  • Scroll up — older messages load automatically as you scroll, all the way back to "Start of conversation".

Opening a thread automatically marks its messages as read and clears the red badge.

images.alt: An open SMS thread showing the header with the peer number, message bubbles with delivery status and the reply box below
images.caption: The thread view: header, bubbles with per-message delivery status (and Retry for failed sends), reply box at the bottom.

images.alt: An open SMS thread in dark mode showing the header, message bubbles with delivery status and the reply box
images.caption: The thread view: header, bubbles with per-message delivery status (and Retry for failed sends), reply box at the bottom.

steps.title: Reply — with one-click templates, emoji, and photos
steps.content:
The reply box at the bottom of every thread is built for speed:

  1. Click a Quick: template chip — like Tech on the way, Running late, or Confirm appointment — and a ready-made message drops into the box with the customer's and technician's details already filled in. You can add your own chips under Dispatch Settings → Messages Templates.
  2. Or type your message in the Type your reply... box. Use the smiley icon for emoji and the paperclip to attach a photo or file — attachments go out as MMS, and your text becomes the caption.
  3. Click Send Reply (or press Ctrl+Enter).

Replies automatically go out on the same business number the thread lives on, so the customer always sees a consistent number. On a group thread, one reply fans out to every participant.

💡 Want to quote a specific message? Hover it, click Reply, and your response goes out with the quoted text on top — great for answering an older question in a busy thread.

images.alt: The reply box with Quick template chips, the reply text area, emoji and attachment buttons and the Send Reply button
images.caption: The reply box: Quick template chips, emoji + attachment buttons, and Send Reply (Ctrl+Enter works too).

images.alt: The reply box in dark mode with Quick template chips, text area, emoji and attachment buttons and Send Reply
images.caption: The reply box: Quick template chips, emoji + attachment buttons, and Send Reply (Ctrl+Enter works too).

steps.title: Start a brand-new conversation
steps.content:
Reaching out first? Click the green New Message button at the top-right.

  1. In the New SMS Message window, pick who you're texting under Send To: Technician, Contact, Lead, or a raw Phone Number.
  2. Choose the person from the searchable dropdown — their saved phone number(s) auto-fill the Destination Phone Number field. No saved number? Type one manually.
  3. Pick your From Number — the 10DLC-registered business line the text is sent from. iMash pre-selects the number you most recently used with that person.
  4. Write your text in Message Body — the paperclip and emoji buttons work here too, and quick template chips appear below for recipients linked to a CRM record.
  5. Click Send Message. The new thread appears at the top of your inbox.
⚠️ US carriers require your texting numbers to be 10DLC-registered — the From Number list only shows registered numbers. If it's empty, register a number first (see the Phone Numbers guide below). If you've connected WhatsApp, a Channel toggle also appears here so the same composer can send via WhatsApp instead.

images.alt: The New SMS Message modal with Send To options, Destination Phone Number, From Number and Message Body fields
images.caption: The composer: pick a recipient type, destination and From number, write the message, hit Send Message.

images.alt: The New SMS Message modal in dark mode with Send To options, Destination Phone Number, From Number and Message Body fields
images.caption: The composer: pick a recipient type, destination and From number, write the message, hit Send Message.

steps.title: Clean up with bulk actions
steps.content:
Inbox piling up? The Bulk button in the filter bar handles many conversations at once:

  1. Click Bulk → Select conversations — checkboxes appear on every card.
  2. Tick the threads you want (or use the Select all checkbox that appears above the list).
  3. Open Bulk again and choose an action:
  • Mark selected as read — clears the unread badges on the ticked threads.
  • Mark all visible as read — one click clears every thread currently matching your filters (the count shows how many).
  • Archive selected — hides the ticked conversations from the inbox. Archiving never deletes the messages; the thread reappears if the person texts you again.

Click Exit multi-select when you're done.

images.alt: The Bulk dropdown menu with Select conversations, Mark selected as read, Mark all visible as read and Archive selected
images.caption: The Bulk menu: multi-select, mark-as-read, and archive actions.

images.alt: The Bulk dropdown menu in dark mode with Select conversations, mark-as-read and Archive selected options
images.caption: The Bulk menu: multi-select, mark-as-read, and archive actions.

steps.title: Good to know: group texts, AI agents, and opt-outs
steps.content:
Three powerful behaviors happen behind the scenes of this inbox:

  • Group MMS threads. When several people share one text thread (for example, a lead-source company's group chat), it shows as a single conversation with a purple team icon. Your reply goes to everyone in the group, and the Add button in the thread header lets you add another participant (they join on your next message). Group threads are SMS/MMS only.
  • AI text agents. If you've bound a Smart Agent to one of your numbers (in the agent's Channels tab), inbound texts on that number get answered by the AI automatically — and the whole conversation streams into this same inbox in real time. You can jump in and reply manually at any moment; the thread stays unified, so you always see both the AI's messages and your own.
  • Opt-outs and compliance. Recipients can always reply STOP to stop receiving texts — carriers enforce this at the network level, and iMash can append an opt-out notice to outbound texts to keep your 10DLC campaigns compliant. Keep business texting on registered numbers and honor opt-outs to protect your delivery rates.

That's the full tour — you now run a professional, multi-channel texting operation from a single screen. 🚀

support.title: Need Help?
support.content: Questions about SMS delivery, 10DLC registration, or a thread that isn't behaving? Email support@imash.io and our team will dig in with you.
